QUANTUM TRAINING
# From theory to practical training
[![](https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Arrow-White.png)](https://kiutra.com/quantum-training#intro)
QUANTUM TRAINING
## From theory to practical training
[![](https://kiutra.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Arrow-White.png)](https://kiutra.com/products/quantum-training#intro)
## The missing link in quantum education
Record growth and breakthrough advances in quantum technologies are driving increasing demand for a skilled quantum workforce. Universities are expanding quantum curricula, but hands-on experimental training often lags behind theory. To prepare the next generation, institutions need to provide hands-on access to low-temperature technologies that support modern quantum education. 
Yet traditional cryogenic systems are complex, bulky, and designed for expert research environments. They require specialized training, significant infrastructure, and careful supervision, making them difficult to integrate into structured teaching labs or student-led experiments.
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20524%20998'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
## The L-Type Rapid for quantum training
#### Rapid cooldown and simple operation
[The L-Type Rapid](https://kiutra.com/products/l-type-rapid-cryostat/) is designed to remove the operational barriers that traditionally limit access to cryogenic experiments. Its fully automatic operation significantly reduces the complexity typically associated with cryogenic measurements, making it possible for students to operate the system independently, without constant expert supervision. With its record fast cooldown of less than 4 hours to millikelvin temperatures, students can prepare, run, and complete a real quantum experiment within a single day in the lab.
#### Flexible puck system for teaching and research
At the core of the [L-Type Rapid](https://kiutra.com/products/l-type-rapid-cryostat/) is its puck-based sample loader. Experiments are mounted on pucks that can be swapped quickly, allowing switching between different teaching content from day to day. This approach makes the system exceptionally flexible: it can serve as a teaching platform during scheduled courses and transition seamlessly into a research instrument outside of teaching hours. By combining simplicity with research-grade measurement capability, the L-Type Rapid becomes an efficient shared resource for both education and active research environments.
## Putting practical experience at the forefront
### How the L-Type Rapid enables quantum training at the University of Copenhagen
## A day in a modern quantum training lab
![Illustration showing: 
9am sample preparation
10am Lecture
12pm Lunch break
1pm Script preparation
2pm Measurement
5pm Finish](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20688%20890'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
With the L-Type Rapid, low-temperature experiments fit naturally into a structured teaching schedule. What once required days of preparation and expert supervision can now be integrated into a single lab session.  
  
In the morning, students prepare the experiment on the puck and initiate the automated cooldown. 
The cooldown time can be used to teach the theoretical foundations and experimental techniques required for the experiment. 
After lunch, measurement scripts and electronics are prepared, and students perform hands-on experiments collecting and analyzing real low-temperature data before the day concludes.  
  
The result is a complete experimental cycle within one day: preparation, theory, measurement, and interpretation all carried out by students themselves.
![In-depth understanding](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20528%20212'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
Deepen knowledge of quantum theory by connecting conceptual principles to real experimental results
![Practical experience](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20527%20212'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
Provide the necessary practical experience to prepare students early on for their career in industry and research
![Quantum outreach](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20527%20212'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
Attract more students to your quantum programs and position your institution as a leader in applied quantum education
## What our customers say
![Niels Bohr Institute logo](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20300%20180'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
**Asbjørn Drachmann**  
Research Consultant, Niels Bohr Institute, University of Copenhagen
_“The L-Type Rapid is a great tool for the Quantum Training Lab. It gives very fast access to millikelvin temperatures, it is very easy and fast to exchange between samples in experiments, and it has very low maintenance.”_

<div class="wp-block-cover is-light" id="intro"><span aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-cover__background has-white-background-color has-background-dim-100 has-background-dim"></span><div class="wp-block-cover__inner-container is-layout-constrained wp-block-cover-is-layout-constrained">
<h2 class="wp-block-heading has-text-align-center">The missing link in quantum education</h2>



<div class="wp-block-group"><div class="wp-block-group__inner-container is-layout-constrained wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained">
<div class="wp-block-columns is-layout-flex wp-container-core-columns-is-layout-8f761849 wp-block-columns-is-layout-flex">
<div class="wp-block-column is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow">
<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Record growth and breakthrough advances in quantum technologies are driving increasing demand for a skilled quantum workforce. Universities are expanding quantum curricula, but hands-on experimental training often lags behind theory. To prepare the next generation, institutions need to provide hands-on access to low-temperature technologies that support modern quantum education. </p>
</div>



<div class="wp-block-column is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow">
<p class="wp-block-paragraph">Yet traditional cryogenic systems are complex, bulky, and designed for expert research environments. They require specialized training, significant infrastructure, and careful supervision, making them difficult to integrate into structured teaching labs or student-led experiments.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div></div>
</div></div>

<div class="wp-block-column is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow" style="flex-basis:75%">
<h2 class="wp-block-heading has-black-color has-text-color">The L-Type Rapid for quantum training</h2>



<div style="height:40px" aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div>



<div class="wp-block-group has-black-color has-grey-background-color has-text-color has-background"><div class="wp-block-group__inner-container is-layout-flow wp-block-group-is-layout-flow">
<div class="wp-block-pb-accordion-item c-accordion__item js-accordion-item no-js is-open" data-initially-open="true" data-click-to-close="false" data-auto-close="true" data-scroll="false" data-scroll-offset="0"><h4 id="at-346670" class="c-accordion__title js-accordion-controller" role="button">Rapid cooldown and simple operation</h4><div id="ac-346670" class="c-accordion__content">
<p class="wp-block-paragraph"><a href="https://kiutra.com/products/l-type-rapid-cryostat/">The L-Type Rapid</a> is designed to remove the operational barriers that traditionally limit access to cryogenic experiments. Its fully automatic operation significantly reduces the complexity typically associated with cryogenic measurements, making it possible for students to operate the system independently, without constant expert supervision. With its record fast cooldown of less than 4 hours to millikelvin temperatures, students can prepare, run, and complete a real quantum experiment within a single day in the lab.</p>
</div></div>



<div class="wp-block-pb-accordion-item c-accordion__item js-accordion-item no-js" data-initially-open="false" data-click-to-close="false" data-auto-close="true" data-scroll="false" data-scroll-offset="0"><h4 id="at-346671" class="c-accordion__title js-accordion-controller" role="button">Flexible puck system for teaching and research</h4><div id="ac-346671" class="c-accordion__content">
<p class="wp-block-paragraph">At the core of the <a href="https://kiutra.com/products/l-type-rapid-cryostat/">L-Type Rapid</a> is its puck-based sample loader. Experiments are mounted on pucks that can be swapped quickly, allowing switching between different teaching content from day to day. This approach makes the system exceptionally flexible: it can serve as a teaching platform during scheduled courses and transition seamlessly into a research instrument outside of teaching hours. By combining simplicity with research-grade measurement capability, the L-Type Rapid becomes an efficient shared resource for both education and active research environments.</p>
</div></div>
</div></div>

<div class="wp-block-column is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow">
<p class="wp-block-paragraph">With the L-Type Rapid, low-temperature experiments fit naturally into a structured teaching schedule. What once required days of preparation and expert supervision can now be integrated into a single lab session.<br><br>In the morning, students prepare the experiment on the puck and initiate the automated cooldown. </p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">The cooldown time can be used to teach the theoretical foundations and experimental techniques required for the experiment. </p>



<p class="wp-block-paragraph">After lunch, measurement scripts and electronics are prepared, and students perform hands-on experiments collecting and analyzing real low-temperature data before the day concludes.<br><br>The result is a complete experimental cycle within one day: preparation, theory, measurement, and interpretation all carried out by students themselves.</p>
</div>
